title: Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia
chief_complaint: Former 26-week premature infant, now 4 months old (corrected 2 months). On home oxygen 0.5L via nasal cannula. Presented with increased work of breathing, increased oxygen requirement to 2L, and poor feeding for 2 days.

physical_exam.Lungs: Tachypnea, retractions, coarse crackles bilaterally, mild wheezing. On 2L O2 via NC.
physical_exam.Cardiac: Tachycardia, loud P2 (pulmonary hypertension). Hepatomegaly 3cm (right heart failure).

hidden_data.cxr: Hyperinflation. Diffuse hazy opacities. Cystic changes in both lungs. Areas of atelectasis alternating with hyperinflation.
hidden_data.echo: Moderate pulmonary hypertension (RVSP 55mmHg). RV hypertrophy. Normal LV function.
hidden_data.abg: pH 7.33, PaCO2 62, PaO2 48, HCO3 32 (chronic compensated respiratory acidosis with acute decompensation)

correct_diagnosis: Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (Severe BPD) with Acute-on-Chronic Respiratory Failure due to RSV Infection, Pulmonary Hypertension, and Right Heart Failure
key_learning_points: BPD = chronic lung disease of prematurity. Definition: Oxygen requirement at 36 weeks corrected gestational age. Pathophysiology: Arrested lung development + volutrauma + oxygen toxicity + inflammation in premature lung
Severity: Mild (RA at 36 weeks), Moderate (<30% O2 at 36 weeks), Severe (>30% O2 or positive pressure at 36 weeks). This patient has severe BPD with pulmonary hypertension

BPD is a multisystem disease: Lungs (alveolar simplification, airway hyperreactivity), Heart (pulmonary hypertension, cor pulmonale), Growth (increased metabolic demand, feeding difficulties), Neurodevelopment (hypoxic episodes)

Acute exacerbations common with viral infections (RSV, rhinovirus). Present with increased O2 requirement, increased work of breathing, feeding intolerance. Treatment: Increase O2, Diuretics (Furosemide 1mg/kg IV/PO BID), Bronchodilators if wheezing, Systemic steroids for severe exacerbations (short course), Antibiotics if suspected bacterial superinfection

Chronic management: Home O2 (target SpO2 92-95%), Diuretics (Furosemide, Thiazides, Spironolactone for pulmonary HTN), Nutrition (high-calorie, fluid restriction 120-140mL/kg/day), Palivizumab for RSV prophylaxis in RSV season, Vaccines (influenza, pneumococcal). Pulmonary vasodilators for PH (Sildenafil). Wean O2 as tolerated with sleep studies. Prognosis: Most improve with age as new lung tissue grows, but have reduced exercise tolerance and earlier COPD as adults
